assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30001.md states 'Classification: Code Change Request' and concludes 'Classification: code change request -- triggers sub-task creation.' The report table shows comment 30001 with classification 'Code change request' and action 'Sub-task created'. subtask-30001.md exists with the sub-task description.

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as suggestion — the reviewer uses suggestive language ('should also', 'would help') and the classification output (review-30002.md) explains this reasoning
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md states 'Classification: Suggestion' and reasoning explicitly references the suggestive language: '"should also add" -- the word "also" indicates this is an additional enhancement beyond the core requirement' and '"would help" -- conditional/suggestive framing indicating this is a recommendation, not a requirement'. Both 'should also' and 'would help' are cited as evidence for suggestion classification.

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30003.md states 'Classification: Nit' and concludes 'Classification: nit -- no sub-task created.' The report table shows comment 30003 classified as 'Nit' with action 'No sub-task (minor error message wording)'. No subtask-30003.md file exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30004.md states 'Classification: Question' and concludes 'Classification: question -- no sub-task created.' The report table shows comment 30004 classified as 'Question' with action 'No sub-task (asks for clarification of intent)'. No subtask-30004.md file exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains all required sections: '## Repository' (line 1: 'trustify-backend'), '## Description' (line 7), '## Files to Modify' (line 19), '## Implementation Notes' (line 22), and '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 29). Only one sub-task was created (subtask-30001.md), and it contains all required sections.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Target PR' section (line 11) with the exact URL 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744' (line 12).

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Review Context' section (line 14) with Comment ID 30001, Author reviewer-a, File reference, and the full original comment text: 'The `soft_delete` method should run all three UPDATE statements inside a single database transaction...'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md), one sub-task description (subtask-30001.md), and a report (report.md). No code files (.rs, .toml, etc.) are present in the outputs. The report's findings are analytical/observational, not code modifications.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All four review comments (30001, 30002, 30003, 30004) have corresponding classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md). The report's 'Review Comment Classifications' table lists all four comments with their classifications and actions. The report's Review Feedback check states '1 code change request (comment 30001: transaction wrapping), 1 suggestion, 1 nit, 1 question' — accounting for all four comments.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: The report's overall result is 'FAIL' with issues requiring attention. There is no mention of merging, auto-merge, or any merge action in the report. The report concludes with analytical findings and root-cause analysis only.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: The report table contains: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | New test file `tests/api/sbom_delete.rs` adds 5 integration tests covering delete, not-found, conflict, include_deleted listing, and cascade behavior |'

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains a 'Convention upgrade check' paragraph: 'The repository has a `CONVENTIONS.md` file at its root. However, without access to its actual content in this verification context, there is no documented evidence that index creation on filter columns is a project convention. The repo-backend key conventions mention SeaORM, Axum, error handling, endpoint registration, and testing patterns -- none reference index creation conventions. Without concrete CONVENTIONS.md evidence or demonstrated codebase pattern counts, this suggestion cannot be upgraded to a code change request.'

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) does NOT result in a sub-task — the suggestion classification is correct (suggestive language, no directive) and no project convention in the fixture data backs an upgrade from suggestion to code change request
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md concludes 'Classification: suggestion -- no sub-task created.' No subtask-30002.md file exists in the outputs directory. The report table shows comment 30002 with action 'No sub-task (no documented convention evidence for index creation)'. The convention upgrade check explicitly states the suggestion cannot be upgraded.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: The report's Test Quality row states: 'Eval Quality: N/A'. The full text is 'Test Quality | PASS | Repetitive Test Detection: PASS (5 tests with distinct behaviors, not parameterization candidates); Test Documentation: PASS (all test functions have doc comments); Eval Quality: N/A'. Eval Quality is listed as N/A and does not affect the overall Test Quality PASS result.
